title: Help Make Belvedere #1 in Safety
description: For a community that often leads the way, it’s surprising that Belvedere ranks near the bottom—10th out of 12 Marin communities—when it comes to AlertMarin registrations. AlertMarin is Marin County’s official emergency notification system.…
content: For a community that often leads the way, it’s surprising that Belvedere ranks near the bottom—10th out of 12 Marin communities—when it comes to AlertMarin registrations. AlertMarin is Marin County’s official emergency notification system. Belvedere Police Chief Jason Wu says it’s our primary tool for reaching residents when it matters most. Whether it’s a wildfire, flood or urgent public safety alert, AlertMarin is how critical information reaches you. At present, approximately 47% of Belvedere households are registered. Increasing participation will ensure that more residents receive critical information when it matters most. Registration is quick and easy, and while one adult per household is the goal, ideally every adult resident should sign up.

title: SIGNAL OUTAGE AT D STREET AND BAYVIEW STREET. TREAT AS A FOUR-WAY STOP. SLOW DOWN AND EXERCISE CAUTION.
description: Due to a powerr outage in the Gerstle Park Neighborhood, the traffic signals are not working at the intersection of D Street and Bayview Street. Intersections where the traffic signals are not working are to be treated as a stop sign. Vehi…
content: Due to a powerr outage in the Gerstle Park Neighborhood, the traffic signals are not working at the intersection of D Street and Bayview Street. Intersections where the traffic signals are not working are to be treated as a stop sign. Vehicle traffic is required to stop and check for hazards, other traffic, and pedestrians before proceeding, it is the law. PG&E is working to restore electrical power but there is not estimate as to when the power will be restored to the neighborhood. Go to PG&E's website to receive updates.

title: The Tiburon Police Department Encourages Safe St. Patrick’s Day Celebrations
description: The Tiburon Police Department Encourages Safe St. Patrick’s Day Celebrations Tiburon, California – As St. Patrick’s Day approaches, Tiburon Police Department reminds everyone that the best way to celebrate the holiday is…
content: The Tiburon Police Department Encourages Safe St. Patrick’s Day Celebrations Tiburon, California – As St. Patrick’s Day approaches, Tiburon Police Department reminds everyone that the best way to celebrate the holiday is to plan ahead for a safe, sober way to get home. According to the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration, 1,355 people were killed in alcohol-impaired traffic crashes in California in 2023. “Our goal is for everyone in Tiburon to enjoy St. Patrick’s Day safely,” said Tiburon Police Chief Michelle Jean. “If you’re celebrating, please plan ahead for a sober ride home so the night ends safely for everyone.” Additional officers will be on patrol March 17, 2026, from 7:00 p.m. to 3:00 a.m. looking for drivers suspected of driving under the influence of alcohol and/or drugs. Plan for a Safe Celebration Before heading out for St. Patrick’s Day festivities, follow these tips to stay safe: Designate a Sober Driver : Choose someone ahead of time who will stay alcohol-free and make sure everyone gets home safely. Use Ridesharing Services, Taxis or Public Transportation : Rideshare apps and transit options are safe alternatives to driving if you've been drinking. Host Responsibly : If you’re hosting, offer plenty of non-alcoholic beverage options and make sure your guests have safe, sober rides home. Look Out for Others : Don’t let friends or family members drive impaired. Take their keys and help arrange a safe ride. Never Drive Impaired or Distracted : It’s not worth the risk – your life and others’ lives depend on it! Impaired driving is not just from alcohol. Prescription drugs, over-the-counter medications, cannabis products and other drugs may also cause impairment. If you plan on drinking or taking medications with a driving warning label, let someone who is sober drive. If you see someone who appears to be driving while impaired, call 9-1-1. Funding for this program is provided by a grant from the California Office of Traffic Safety, through the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration.

title: Our Use of Flock Safety
description: In 2010, Tiburon was one of the first communities in Marin County to implement Automated License Plate Reader (ALPR) cameras at the entry and exit points of the Tiburon peninsula. Last summer, we transitioned away from our prior vendor, Vi…
content: In 2010, Tiburon was one of the first communities in Marin County to implement Automated License Plate Reader (ALPR) cameras at the entry and exit points of the Tiburon peninsula. Last summer, we transitioned away from our prior vendor, Vigilant, and contracted with Flock Safety to replace the ALPR cameras at the same locations. Since last summer, there have been questions with Flock, rooted in the possibility that some California law enforcement agencies may be accessing other agencies' data to share that data with federal immigration agencies, which is a violation of state law (SB34). We are aware of this and agree that this is an unacceptable use of the Town's license plate reader data. The Tiburon Police Department's authorized users of the Flock system are fully compliant with SB34. Our officers have not, and will not, utilize the Flock system to share data in violation of state law. In order to ensure Tiburon's ALPR data is not used unlawfully, we have also implemented the following: ALPR Policy Amendment - Audits: In December 2025, the Town amended its ALPR policy to require monthly audits of its own officers' searches, and audit external agencies' searches of Tiburon's data. The audits require selecting entries at random for review, as well as searching for known terms that would indicate an illegal use, such as 'ICE'. Terminating Access for External Agency Violations: If, in the audit, the Town learns that an external agency may have searched the Town's system for an impermissible reason, the Town provides notice to the agency that it may be in violation of state law, and if no explanation is provided, the agency will lose access to the Town's Flock system. Publishing the "Search" Disclaimer: The Town worked with Flock to ensure any user searching the Town's system must accept the following disclaimer: " By accessing this system, I acknowledge and agree that my use of Flock Safety technology must be in compliance with California law and ... this system may not be used to investigate, interrogate, detain, detect or arrest persons for immigration purposes. " Working with Flock to Customize Searches: The Town met with Flock's legal counsel and project managers to limit how external agencies can search Tiburon's ALPR data. Specifically, the Town worked with Flock to allow external searches only for valid reasons, such as burglary, theft, homicide (i.e. criminal cases). We acknowledge our responsibility to operate a safe and effective ALPR system for the community. We have dedicated a lot of time over the last several months to evaluating our policies and procedures and conducting meaningful user training to ensure compliance with both state law and our local policies. We are continuing to evaluate our data shares. Moving forward, we are now conducting monthly internal (Tiburon Police searches through the system) and external (other agencies' searches through our data) audits. We are publishing the internal audits of the prior month on Tiburon Police's Transparency Portal . We have opted to publish the full internal audit, instead of Flock's automatic 'public audit', because it provides the names of the officers doing the search, whereas Flock's public audit does not. We will continue performing case-by-case redactions of the 'reason for search' column if the search relates to a case under investigation to protect the integrity of the investigation. As part of the external audits, we review the searches external agencies have run through the Town's system. If the search looks to be in violation of state law, we work with that agency to determine the legitimacy of the search. Thus far, we have found these searches were legitimate criminal investigations and not related to immigration enforcement. We have stopped sharing with a few agencies that did not respond to our inquiries. We are not proactively publishing the external audit document, but we will make it available by Public Records Act request. We believe our improved audit protocols strike an appropriate balance between transparency and our responsibilities to protect the integrity of our investigative records, the investigative records of other law enforcement agencies, and the privacy of others. We are working diligently to ensure reasonable guardrails are in place on the Flock system. We have met with Flock, we have updated our policies to make our officers' searches more auditable, and we have taken action to work with other law enforcement agencies. The Tiburon Police Department has taken swift and noteworthy steps to ensure Flock is utilized responsibly in this community. ALPRs are an effective crime solving and prevention tool. There is always room for improvement, but we are confident that we have taken reasonable action to ensure continued legal and responsible use of the system. We will continue to evaluate, adjust, and update our procedures as necessary, and welcome your feedback.

title: Small Business Administration is available at the City Hall Recreation Center for Disaster assistance loans/info.
description: The U.S. Small Business Administration (SBA) will be available at the City Hall Recreation Center from 10:30 AM to 7:00 PM, Tuesday, 2/17, through Friday, 2/20/26, to provide information and assistance with Disaster Assistance Loans follow…
content: The U.S. Small Business Administration (SBA) will be available at the City Hall Recreation Center from 10:30 AM to 7:00 PM, Tuesday, 2/17, through Friday, 2/20/26, to provide information and assistance with Disaster Assistance Loans following the recent high tide flooding. SBA representatives can help residents and businesses apply for low-interest disaster loans, explain eligibility requirements, and answer questions about available recovery programs. Additional information and online applications are available at sba.gov/funding-programs/disaster-assistance.

title: Emergency Notification News
description: Start the new year safer! Now is the perfect time to replace the batteries in all older smoke and carbon monoxide detectors—make this an annual habit. Newer smoke and CO₂ detectors are sealed with 10-year batteries, so check the manufactur…
content: Start the new year safer! Now is the perfect time to replace the batteries in all older smoke and carbon monoxide detectors—make this an annual habit. Newer smoke and CO₂ detectors are sealed with 10-year batteries, so check the manufacture date to be sure yours are still within their lifespan. While you’re in safety mode, verify that you are signed up for emergency alerts through AlertMarin and confirm that your contact information is current. AlertMarin is the only official emergency notification system for Belvedere and Tiburon. Other systems like Nixle do not send disaster alerts for the Tiburon Peninsula. However, Nixle may be useful for receiving traffic updates along the Highway 101 corridor. These free services provide critical, real-time notifications during emergencies. For real-time emergency information, visit the Marin County Emergency Portal .

title: Score Big on Safety: Celebrate Super Bowl Responsibly
description: Plan ahead with a sober ride before the big game As football fans gear up for the excitement of the big game, Novato Police reminds everyone that the best game plan includes getting home safely. Whether you're hosting a party, attending a…
content: Plan ahead with a sober ride before the big game As football fans gear up for the excitement of the big game, Novato Police reminds everyone that the best game plan includes getting home safely. Whether you're hosting a party, attending a gathering, or enjoying the game at your favorite spot, planning ahead with a sober ride home will keep you and everyone on the road safe. “Super Bowl celebrations are a time for fun and community, but safety should always come first,” said Sergeant Trevor Hall “If you plan to drink, make sure you have a reliable way to get home, whether that’s a designated sober driver, rideshare, taxi or public transportation.” During Super Bowl LIX weekend, Novato Police will have officers on patrol looking for drivers suspected of being under the influence of alcohol and/or drugs. Plan for a Safe Celebration Before heading out for Super Bowl festivities, follow these tips to stay safe:

title: Tiburon Police Department Arrests Burglary Suspect
description: FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE Case Number TP26-36 & TP26-37 Tiburon, CA -- On 02/04/2026 at 0027 hours, the Tiburon Police Department responded to the 1700 block of Lagoon View Drive for a reported burglary in progress. The homeowner reported that…
content: FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE Case Number TP26-36 & TP26-37 Tiburon, CA -- On 02/04/2026 at 0027 hours, the Tiburon Police Department responded to the 1700 block of Lagoon View Drive for a reported burglary in progress. The homeowner reported that a male suspect entered his garage and stole a bicycle valued at approximately $10,000 before fleeing on foot. The homeowner pursued the suspect, who abandoned the bicycle approximately 50 yards away and fled. The homeowner recovered the bicycle and provided officers with a detailed suspect description. With assistance from the Marin County Sheriff’s Office, Mill Valley Police Department, and Belvedere Police Department, a perimeter was established and an area search was conducted. Officers located scattered coins, a white hat, and two vehicles in the area that appeared to have been rifled through. At approximately 0152 hours, Tiburon officers located a suspicious vehicle parked on Ridge Road. Officers contacted the male occupant who provided inconsistent statements. Officers noted the subject was wearing clothing consistent with the suspect description provided by the victim and observed on surveillance video. The suspect was positively identified and placed under arrest. A search of the suspect’s vehicle revealed suspected methamphetamine, multiple coins, eight stolen credit cards belonging to another Tiburon resident, and additional evidence linked to nearby thefts from unlocked vehicles. The suspect was booked into the Marin County Jail for burglary, possession of a controlled substance, and possession of stolen property. Due to his prior criminal history and convictions, the suspect is eligible for several enhancements pursuant to Proposition 36. This case has been forwarded to the Marin County District Attorney’s Office. As a reminder, the Tiburon Police Department encourages community members to remain vigilant and report suspicious activity. Residents are reminded to lock and secure their vehicles and homes and take additional precautions to safeguard their property. MEDIA CONTACT: Sergeant Rob Law #303 415-706-0661 RLaw@tiburonpd.org

title: UPDATE- The early morning threat received at Redwood High School has been safely resolved without injury or incident.
description: Earlier this morning at approximately 8am, the Central Marin Police received a call from the staff at Redwood High School advising of a possible threat to the school. Staff advised that an unknown caller left a recorded voice mail message…
content: Earlier this morning at approximately 8am, the Central Marin Police received a call from the staff at Redwood High School advising of a possible threat to the school. Staff advised that an unknown caller left a recorded voice mail message indicating that they were driving to Redwood with a gun and a bomb. Officers immediately responded to the school, sent officers into the school, and established a high visibility perimeter around the campus. Within minutes officers coordinated with district administration to begin evacuating the students that were already on campus. With the assistance of our local partners from the Tamalpais Union High School District, Redwood High School, the Central Marin Fire Department, and the Marin County Sheriff's Office, we were able to evacuate the campus in a matter of minutes without injury or incident. The Central Marin Police conducted an extensive search of the entire Redwood High School campus and determined the campus is safe and no threat exists to the safety of the students or staff. The Central Marin Police take these types of threats very seriously and worked collaboratively to ensure the safety of everyone on campus this morning. We recognize how frightening these events are for our residents and school community and stand alongside you to ensure the safety of our children, schools and communities. The Central Marin Police would like to extend our gratitude to the Tamalpais Union High School District, Redwood High School, the Central Marin Fire Department, and the Marin County Sheriff's Office for their partnership and resources in resolving this incident. The Central Marin Police is currently investigating this criminal threat and following up on leads. When located, the suspect/s responsible will be arrested and prosecuted to the fullest extent of the law.

title: Annual Firearm Safety Reminder from the Belvedere P.D.
description: Securely storing firearms when not in use is an essential part of responsible gun ownership—and the most effective way to help prevent firearm accidents, theft, and misuse. Belvedere Municipal Code 9.7 requires all firearms to be properly…
content: Securely storing firearms when not in use is an essential part of responsible gun ownership—and the most effective way to help prevent firearm accidents, theft, and misuse. Belvedere Municipal Code 9.7 requires all firearms to be properly secured when not in use, and California Penal Code 25100 makes an individual criminally liable if a loaded firearm is stored where a child is likely to gain access. The Belvedere Police Department offers free trigger locks and cable locks that prevent a firearm from operating while secured under lock and key, providing an added level of safety. To obtain a lock, call the Belvedere Police Department at (415) 435-3233 to arrange a time with a police officer. For more information on firearm safety, visit the Belvedere P olice Department webpage .
